name: space-tracker
description: Unified single-object-tracking benchmark across SatSOT, SV248S, and OOTB. Manifest indexes sequences and unified-attribute groupings; raw imagery and GT must be obtained from the original datasets per their license terms.

evaluation.tiny_definition: A sequence is tiny if its median sqrt(GT area) across annotated frames is < 8 px.

ootb.name: OOTB
ootb.paper: Chen et al., ISPRS J. Photogramm. Remote Sens. 2024
ootb.gt_format_description: Per-frame line of 8 floats: x1,y1,x2,y2,x3,y3,x4,y4 — corners of an oriented bounding box.

satsot.name: SatSOT
satsot.gt_format_description: Per-frame line of 4 floats x,y,w,h; lines containing 'none' mark frames where the target is absent.

sv248s.name: SV248S
sv248s.gt_format_description: Per-sequence: <ann_dir>/<seq_id>.rect (xywh per frame) and <ann_dir>/<seq_id>.state (per-frame flag: 0=visible, 1=invisible, 2=occluded). Sequences live at <video_dir>/sequences/<seq_id>/*.tiff with annotations at <video_dir>/annotations/<seq_id>.{rect,state,abs,poly,attr}.

BC.definition: Background has similar appearance (texture/color) to the target.

IV.definition: Illumination around the target changes significantly.

ROT.definition: Target rotates in the image plane (>= 30° for SV248S).

OCC.definition: Target is occluded by scene structures (any degree, any duration). Consolidates spatial (PO/FO/POC/FOC) and temporal (STO/LTO/CO) annotations across the three datasets.

SOB.definition: Nearby objects share shape / type / appearance with the target (≤ 2.5x object size for SV248S).

attribute_taxonomy.description: Full Space-tracker-SOT sequence-attribute taxonomy (paper table: split_attributes_table.tex). Four groups: shared (collapsed unified rows), aspect_ratio (dataset-unique), dataset_unique_other, and occlusion_subtypes (drill-down of the unified OCC row). Each per-sequence ``taxonomy_attrs`` field is the flat list of taxonomy names that sequence carries, derived from ``native_attrs`` via each attribute's ``datasets`` mapping.

shared.description: Attributes annotated in >= 2 of the 3 source datasets, collapsed into one unified row. Same 6 keys as ``unified_attributes``.

aspect_ratio.description: Dataset-unique aspect-ratio attributes. ARC measures temporal change of the aspect ratio (SatSOT); OON measures static extremeness of the aspect ratio (OOTB).

dataset_unique_other.description: Other dataset-unique attributes (annotated by exactly one of the three datasets).

occlusion_subtypes.description: Sub-types of the unified OCC row. SatSOT/OOTB split occlusion spatially (partial vs. full); SV248S splits it temporally (short / long / continuous). The two axes are not comparable at the sequence-attribute level, so we report sub-types here rather than collapsing them.

BC.definition: Background has similar appearance (texture/color) to the target.

IV.definition: Illumination around the target changes significantly.

ROT.definition: Target rotates in the image plane (>= 30° for SV248S).

OCC.definition: Target is occluded by scene structures (any degree, any duration). Consolidates spatial (PO/FO/POC/FOC) and temporal (STO/LTO/CO) annotations across the three datasets.

SOB.definition: Nearby objects share shape / type / appearance with the target (≤ 2.5x object size for SV248S).

ARC.definition: Ratio of the current-frame bbox aspect ratio to the first-frame bbox aspect ratio is outside [0.5, 2] (SatSOT spec).

OON.definition: The bounding-box aspect ratio itself is outside [0.3, 3] in the sequence.

LQ.definition: Image quality is low; target is hard to distinguish.

BJT.definition: Background jitter caused by satellite camera shaking.

BCH.definition: Background has noticeable changes in color or texture along the sequence.

ND.definition: Target appearance affected by smog, sand, or clouds.

IBG.definition: Target disappears without occluder (too similar to surroundings) for >= 10 frames. Renamed from SV248S's BCL to avoid confusion with the frame-level INV flag of which BCL is the sequence-level aggregation.

SM.definition: Target moves slowly (< 2.2 pps in SV248S).

LT.definition: Target has poor texture information, causing discrimination difficulty.

MB.definition: Target region is blurred due to object or platform motion.

IM.definition: Nearby objects move with similar magnitude and direction.

AM.definition: Nearby objects move with similar magnitude but opposite direction.

POC.definition: Target is partially (but not fully) occluded in the image plane. Merges SatSOT's POC with OOTB's PO.

FOC.definition: Target is fully occluded in the image plane. Merges SatSOT's FOC with OOTB's FO.

STO.definition: <= 50 consecutive frames carry the OCC flag (SV248S spec).

LTO.definition: > 50 consecutive frames carry the OCC flag (SV248S spec).

CO.definition: Two or more STO/LTO events occur within a single sequence (SV248S spec).
